competency_groups.title: Sets and Functions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to set theory, relations, functions, and trigonometric identities.
competencies: Represent sets using roster and set-builder notation; identify empty, finite, infinite, equal, subset, universal sets.
Perform operations on sets: union, intersection, difference, complement; apply properties of complements.
Draw Venn diagrams for set operations.
Define relations as subsets of Cartesian products; determine domain, co-domain, and range from pictorial or algebraic representations.

Distinguish functions from general relations; identify real-valued functions (constant, identity, polynomial, rational, modulus, signum, exponential, logarithmic, greatest integer) with their domains and ranges.
Graph standard real-valued functions and perform operations on them: sum, difference, product, quotient.
Define ordered pairs and Cartesian products; calculate the number of elements in finite Cartesian products.
Measure angles in degrees and radians; convert between measures using unit circle definitions.
Apply trigonometric identities including sin²x + cos²x = 1; determine signs and graphs of trigonometric functions.
Express sin(x ± y) and cos(x ± y) in terms of individual components; derive sum-to-product and product-to-sum formulas.
Derive double angle (sin 2x, cos 2x, tan 2x) and triple angle identities for sine, cosine, and tangent.

competency_groups.title: Sets and Functions (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Solve practical problems involving union and intersection of two sets.

competency_groups.title: Trigonometric Equations (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Find the general solution of trigonometric equations: sin y = sin a, cos y = cos a, tan y = tan a.

competency_groups.title: Algebra (Complex Numbers & Induction)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to complex numbers, quadratic equations, and mathematical induction.
competencies: Motivate the need for complex numbers due to unsolvable quadratic equations in real systems; define √-1.
Apply algebraic properties of complex numbers (addition, subtraction, multiplication).
Represent complex numbers on the Argand plane and use polar representation.
State the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra; solve quadratic equations with real coefficients in the complex number system.

competency_groups.title: Mathematical Induction
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the principle of mathematical induction.
competencies: Explain the process of proof by induction; motivate it using natural numbers as the least inductive subset of real numbers.

competency_groups.title: Linear Inequalities (Graphical)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to graphical solutions of linear inequalities.
competencies: Solve linear inequalities in one variable algebraically and represent them on the number line.

competency_groups.title: Systems of Linear Inequalities (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Solve linear inequalities in two variables graphically.

competency_groups.title: Binomial Theorem (General & Middle Terms) (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Identify the general term and middle terms in binomial expansions.

competency_groups.title: Special Sums (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Apply formulae for special sums: Σk, Σk², Σk³.

competency_groups.title: Permutations and Combinations
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to counting principles.
competencies: Apply the fundamental principle of counting.

competency_groups.title: Binomial Theorem (Statement & Proof)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the binomial theorem for positive integral indices.
competencies: State and prove the Binomial Theorem for positive integral indices.

competency_groups.title: Sequence and Series (A.M. & G.P.)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to arithmetic means and geometric progressions.
competencies: Define Arithmetic Mean (A.M.).

competency_groups.title: Geometric Progression Formulas
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to G.P. formulas.
competencies: Derive and apply the general term of a Geometric Progression (G.P.).

competency_groups.title: Sum of G.P. Terms
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to summing geometric progressions.
competencies: Calculate the sum of n terms and infinite sums (for |r| < 1) of a Geometric Progression.

competency_groups.title: Geometric Mean & AM-GM Relation
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to geometric mean.
competencies: Define Geometric Mean (G.M.) and establish the relation between A.M. and G.M.

competency_groups.title: Straight Lines (Recall & Forms)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to recall of 2D geometry and forms of line equations.
competencies: Calculate the slope of a line and the angle between two lines.

competency_groups.title: Equations of a Line (Various Forms)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to various forms of line equations.
competencies: Write and apply the equation of a line in: parallel to axis, point-slope form, slope-intercept form, two-point form, intercept form.

competency_groups.title: Distance of a Point from a Line
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to distance calculations.
competencies: Calculate the perpendicular distance of a point from a given line.

competency_groups.title: Normal Form (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Write the normal form of a line equation.

competency_groups.title: General Equation (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Derive and use the general equation of a line.

competency_groups.title: Conic Sections (Classification & Equations)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to conic sections.
competencies: Classify conic sections: circle, ellipse, parabola, hyperbola; identify degenerate cases.

competency_groups.title: Standard Equations of Conics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to standard equations.
competencies: State and apply the standard equation of a circle.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Ellipse, Parabola, Hyperbola
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to properties.
competencies: Apply simple properties and standard equations for parabola, ellipse, and hyperbola.

competency_groups.title: Three-dimensional Geometry (Axes & Planes)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to 3D coordinate systems.
competencies: Identify and describe the axes and planes in three dimensions.

competency_groups.title: Coordinates and Distance in 3D
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to coordinates.
competencies: Determine the coordinates of a point in three dimensions.

competency_groups.title: Distance Formula (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Calculate the distance between two points in three dimensions.

competency_groups.title: Section Formula (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Apply the section formula.

competency_groups.title: Limits and Derivatives (Introduction)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the introduction of calculus concepts.
competencies: Introduce derivative as rate of change in distance functions.

competency_groups.title: Derivative as Tangent Slope (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Relate the derivative to the slope of the tangent to a curve.

competency_groups.title: Limit Concepts (Polynomials & Rational)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to limits.
competencies: Apply intuitive ideas of limit.

competency_groups.title: Limits of Standard Functions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to limits.
competencies: Evaluate limits for polynomial, rational, trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions.

competency_groups.title: Derivative Definition & Operations
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to derivative definition.
competencies: Define the derivative formally.

competency_groups.title: Differentiation Rules (Sum, Diff, Prod, Quotient)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to differentiation rules.
competencies: Apply derivative rules for sum, difference, product, and quotient of functions.

competency_groups.title: Derivatives of Polynomial & Trigonometric Functions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to specific function derivatives.
competencies: Differentiate polynomial and trigonometric functions.

competency_groups.title: Derivative of Composite Functions (Chain Rule) (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Apply the chain rule to differentiate composite functions.

competency_groups.title: Measures of Dispersion (Ungrouped Data)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to dispersion measures.
competencies: Calculate range, mean deviation, variance, and standard deviation for ungrouped data.

competency_groups.title: Measures of Dispersion (Grouped Data)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to dispersion measures.
competencies: Calculate range, mean deviation, variance, and standard deviation for grouped data.

competency_groups.title: Types of Events (Occurrence, Not, And, Or)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to basic event types.
competencies: Identify occurrence of events; define 'not', 'and', and 'or' events.

competency_groups.title: Event Classifications (Exhaustive, Mutually Exclusive)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to event classifications.
competencies: Distinguish between exhaustive and mutually exclusive events.

competency_groups.title: Axiomatic (Set Theoretic) Probability
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to axiomatic probability.
competencies: Apply the axioms of set-theoretic probability.

competency_groups.title: Axiomatic Probability Connections (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Establish connections between axiomatic probability and theories from earlier classes.

competency_groups.title: Calculating Probabilities of Events (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Calculate probabilities of 'not', 'and', and 'or' events.

competency_groups.title: Random Experiments & Sample Space (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Additional competencies for formative assessment only.
competencies: Identify random experiments, outcomes, and represent sample spaces using set notation.
